topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink swam before my eyes. A C-minus. It was an impossible number, a complete betrayal of the hours I’d poured into that essay. I stared at the printed grade, the edges of the paper seeming to vibrate under my gaze. My hands, which had been steady while writing, now felt clumsy and cold. I slowly folded the paper, the crisp sound echoing loudly in the otherwise silent study room. A knot tightened in my stomach, a heavy, unpleasant pressure that made it hard to draw a full breath. I picked up my pen, but the ink seemed to smear as I tried to focus on the next task, the world outside the window suddenly too bright, too sharp.

[story 2]

"A D?" I whispered, the sound barely audible above the hum of the ventilation system. The professor’s feedback, a sparse collection of critical annotations, felt like a personal indictment. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning in protest beneath my weight. My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. I rubbed my temples, trying to smooth away the sudden, dizzying swirl of anxiety. Every sentence I’d crafted, every late night fueled by lukewarm coffee, seemed to dissolve into meaningless scribbles. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of my desk, the floorboards seeming to shift beneath my feet as I searched frantically for an explanation that simply wasn't there.

[story 3]

I reread the feedback three times, each pass confirming the same disheartening assessment. A solid B- was what I’d anticipated, the kind of grade that signaled genuine understanding. This... this felt like a failure of comprehension, a fundamental misunderstanding of the material. A flush crept up my neck, spreading across my cheeks. I picked up my backpack, the weight suddenly feeling immense and burdensome. I walked to the door, my steps slow and deliberate, as if moving through thick water. When I finally opened it, the hallway seemed overwhelmingly vast, and I felt a sudden, desperate urge to just retreat, to disappear into the anonymity of the crowded corridor.
